sueno-higiene.title: Set up rest early
sueno-higiene.body: Dim screens and bright light an hour before bed, and keep the room cool (~18-19 °C). What steadies sleep most is going to bed and waking up at similar times.

sueno-nsdr.title: Slept little? Recover in the afternoon
sueno-nsdr.body: 10-20 minutes of deep rest without sleeping (NSDR / yoga nidra) in the afternoon help restore some of the rest you missed, without replacing night sleep.

estres-suspiro.title: Physiological sigh to dial it down
estres-suspiro.body: Two short inhales in a row through the nose, then one long exhale through the mouth. Repeat 3-4 times: it lowers your heart rate within seconds.

estres-luz.title: Natural light in the morning
estres-luz.body: About 10-30 minutes of natural light early on help set your internal clock and ease the feeling of stress through the day.

fatiga-estimulos.title: Don't stack stimulants every day
fatiga-estimulos.body: Strong caffeine + pre-workout + intense music every session flattens your baseline motivation over time. Save those for the days you really need them.

dolor-frio.title: Cold for aches, but not right after training
dolor-frio.body: Gentle cold can soothe, but keep it several hours apart from volume training: right after, it blunts part of the adaptation you're after.

dolor-coach.title: Pain that won't ease → speak up
dolor-coach.body: If the pain is localized or won't let up, tell your coach before loading heavy. Taking care of yourself today is training better tomorrow.

humor-movimiento.title: Move a little to lift your mood
humor-movimiento.body: Natural light and some gentle movement (even a short walk) lift your mood more than staying still.

motivacion-arranca.title: Motivation comes after you start
motivacion-arranca.body: Don't wait to feel like it before starting: do the first light set and let your body find its rhythm. The rest almost always follows.

gen-ok.title: You're in your range — keep it up
gen-ok.body: Good day to follow the plan as it comes. Keep your sleep and morning light consistent so this streak holds.

gen-warn.title: How to sleep better tonight
gen-warn.body: Dim the light and screens an hour before bed and keep the room cool (~18-19 °C). Holding your schedule is what improves recovery most.

gen-alert.title: Reset your nervous system
gen-alert.body: Physiological sigh: two short inhales through the nose and one long exhale through the mouth, 3-4 times. If you can, add 10-20 min of deep rest (NSDR) in the afternoon.

gen-descanso-series.title: Long rest between sets pays off
gen-descanso-series.body: In heavy work, resting 3-5 minutes between sets isn't wasting time: it lets you move more weight with better technique.

tipSource: Based on science communication about sleep, stress and recovery

fatiga.label: Fatigue
fatiga.q: How tired do you feel today?

motivacion.label: Motivation
motivacion.q: How much drive do you have?

Búlgaro.name: Bulgarian
Búlgaro.character: Only the competition lifts and squats, at heavy singles, every day. Zero variety: adaptation IS specificity.

Ruso.name: Russian
Ruso.character: Volume undulation over a wide general base: pulls at 90–110%, lots of squatting, complexes in the base and classic peaking.

Chino.name: Chinese
Chino.character: Squat-dominant technique-strength hybrid: power variants and segments, snatch balance and sots, and EVERY session closes with a functional bodybuilding block.

Cubano.name: Cuban
Cubano.character: Speed-strength through complexes and power/hang variants, at moderate percentages and maximum quality per set.

Colombiano.name: Colombian
Colombiano.character: Absolute priority on the clean & jerk with extreme leg volume (front-squat dominant); at peaking the legs drop to zero and only the lift remains.

Coreano.name: Korean
Coreano.character: Rigid structure with heavy pulls everywhere (including from blocks) and position work: strength is built in the pull.

Polaco.name: Polish
Polaco.character: Quality over quantity in short cycles: singles and doubles at high % from early on, with pulls from blocks as the second pillar.

Ucraniano.name: Ukrainian
Ucraniano.character: Density: few pieces per session, doubles and triples EMOM-style, zero slow accessories — rest is the variable being trained.

Híbrido.name: Hybrid
Híbrido.character: Accumulation→transmutation→realization blocks with big compound lifts and complexes for time efficiency — maximum stimulus in a short session.

USA.name: USA
USA.character: American linear 50:50 general strength : Olympic specificity — power variants dominant in the base, complexes in the build, back accessories always.

accumulation.label: Accumulation
accumulation.rationale: High-volume week: this is where you build the stimulus that later turns into strength.

intensification.label: Intensification
intensification.rationale: The last useful kilos of strength: volume drops, intensity rises.

peak.label: Peak
peak.rationale: We fine-tune at competition weight. Less work, same weight.

taper.label: Taper
taper.rationale: We shed fatigue without losing strength: rest is half the work.

comp_week.label: Competition week
comp_week.rationale: Openers only: we dissipate fatigue so you arrive sharp on competition day.

deload.label: Deload
deload.rationale: Deload: this is where the body rebuilds and comes back stronger.

green.headline: Readiness green
green.rationale: Good recovery: the plan as written is allowed.

amber.headline: Readiness amber
amber.rationale: Partial recovery: intensity held, no raising the top; volume trimmed to ~90%.

red.headline: Readiness red
red.rationale: Low recovery: dose trimmed (~75% of volume).

readinessNone.headline: No readiness signal
readinessNone.rationale: No recent monitoring to modulate this week.

readinessMoveSingles: Move the heavy singles, don't delete them.

bulgaro-6d.name: Bulgarian 6D
bulgaro-6d.desc: Daily Max. Maximum specificity, extreme intensity, high frequency.
bulgaro-6d.bestFor: Advanced athletes with a CNS adapted to >90% daily.

coreano-5d.name: Korean 5D
coreano-5d.desc: Rigid structure, positional strength, discipline. Single session.
coreano-5d.bestFor: Emphasis on pulls and transition positions.

coreano-6d.name: Korean 6D
coreano-6d.desc: High-density Korean. More volume spread across 6 days.
coreano-6d.bestFor: Full-time athletes with good recovery capacity.

chino-5d.name: Chinese 5D
chino-5d.desc: Strength-technique hybrid + functional bodybuilding. Pulls and squats.

cubano-novicio-2d.name: Cuban Novice 2D
cubano-novicio-2d.desc: Introduction with 2 weekly sessions. Technical focus.

cubano-novicio-3d.name: Cuban Novice 3D
cubano-novicio-3d.desc: Efficient split: volume Monday, technique Wednesday, intensity Friday.

cubano-int-5d.name: Cuban Intermediate 5D
cubano-int-5d.desc: Mon volume · Wed mid intensity · Fri test. Classic structure.
cubano-int-5d.bestFor: Athlete with an established technical base.

cubano-avanzado-5d.name: Cuban Advanced 5D
cubano-avanzado-5d.desc: Greater work capacity. Monthly volume 1,100-1,300 reps.

cubano-competidor.name: Cuban Competitor
cubano-competidor.desc: Macrocycle oriented to a competitive peak. Accumulation → realization.

polaco-4d.name: Polish 4D
polaco-4d.desc: Short shock cycle. Cautious progression + frequent peaks.
polaco-4d.bestFor: Athletes who need to peak fast without burning out.

polaco-5d.name: Polish 5D
polaco-5d.desc: Quality over quantity. Short sets (1-3 reps), lots of pulls from blocks.

ruso-5d.name: Russian 5D
ruso-5d.desc: Waviness: daily + weekly undulation. Extensive GPP.
ruso-5d.bestFor: Classic athlete, long cycle with a final peak.

ucraniano-3d.name: Ukrainian 3D
ucraniano-3d.desc: Pure high density. EMOM-heavy, ideal for CrossFitters.

ucraniano-4d.name: Ukrainian 4D
ucraniano-4d.desc: Standard. More accessory volume and load distribution.

colombiano-5d.name: Colombian 5D
colombiano-5d.desc: Absolute priority on the C&J. Mesocycles with constant undulation.
colombiano-5d.bestFor: Athletes with a weak jerk who need to reinforce overhead.

hibrido-3d.name: Modern Hybrid 3D
hibrido-3d.desc: High density per session. Big compounds, fewer accessories.

hibrido-4d.name: Modern Hybrid 4D
hibrido-4d.desc: Block periodization. Hypertrophy + strength + EMOM density.

hibrido-block.name: Modular Hybrid (BLOCK)
hibrido-block.desc: Coordinated variation. Concentrated blocks with weekly undulation.

usa-school.name: USA Weightlifting
usa-school.desc: American linear periodization. Volume / intensity balance.

usa-principiante.name: USA Beginner
usa-principiante.desc: 16-week linear periodization: anatomical prep → base strength → integration → intensification and test.
usa-principiante.bestFor: Beginners (0-2 years) building a technical and strength base.

usa-intermedio.name: USA Intermediate
usa-intermedio.desc: 16-week undulating blocks: accumulation → transmutation → intensification → realization, with deloads.
usa-intermedio.bestFor: Intermediates (2-5 years) with a solid base.

usa-avanzado.name: USA Advanced
usa-avanzado.desc: 8-week pre-competition peaking: transmutative overload → neural shock → submaximal realization → tapering.
usa-avanzado.bestFor: Advanced athletes sharpening for a competition.

usa-master.name: USA Master 40+
usa-master.desc: Adapted to 12 weeks for masters: joint stability → preserved strength → realization, with long rests.
usa-master.bestFor: Master athletes (40+) prioritizing joints and recovery.
